label: Easy
description: Missing CSRF token validation on state-changing endpoint.
summary: A PR adds a delete endpoint for user accounts but forgets to validate CSRF tokens, allowing attackers to trick users into deleting their accounts.
issue_title: Add CSRF protection to delete endpoint
issue_body: The new delete account endpoint is vulnerable to CSRF attacks because it doesn't validate CSRF tokens. An attacker could create a malicious page that makes authenticated users delete their accounts.

diffs.routes/account.py:
@@
+@router.post("/account/delete")
+def delete_account(user=Depends(get_current_user)):
+    if not user:
+        raise HTTPException(status_code=401)
+    db.delete_user(user.id)
+    return {"success": True}

files.middleware/csrf.py:
from fastapi import Request, HTTPException

def validate_csrf_token(request: Request) -> bool:
    """Validate CSRF token from request."""
    token = request.headers.get("X-CSRF-Token")
    session_token = request.session.get("csrf_token")
    return token and token == session_token
